vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-opus-4.8
vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF: make the Settings > Units selection explicitly indicate that it also controls the temperature unit (closes #23)

plan.title: Indicate temperature follows the Units selection

narratives.Overview: In Settings, the Units section presents the imperial/metric toggle only in terms of speed & distance, but the ride-screen temperature also follows this selection (imperial -> F, metric -> C). That coupling is currently invisible to the user (GitHub issue #23).
narratives.Problem: Users cannot tell from the UI that the Units choice also changes the temperature unit, which is undiscoverable and potentially confusing.
narratives.Goals:
1. Make it explicit that the Units selection controls temperature as well as speed and distance.
2. Keep the existing coupled behavior (no independent temperature override in this scope).
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: The Units picker MUST communicate that it controls speed, distance, AND temperature.
FR-2: Each option MUST concretely show the temperature scale it implies (Imperial -> F, Metric -> C).
FR-3: No change to the underlying behavior: temperature still follows speedUnit.temperatureUnit.
narratives.Architecture: Add a pure settingsOptionLabel to SpeedUnit that names all controlled measurements, e.g. 'Imperial (mph/mi, F)' / 'Metric (km/h/km, C)'. Update SettingsView's Units section to use that label, rename the picker row to include Temperature, and add a section footer stating temperature follows the selection.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit-test SpeedUnit.settingsOptionLabel for both cases (contains speed, distance, and temperature symbols). The footer/picker presentation is verified by build + on-device review.

narrative.Acceptance: SpeedUnit exposes a label that includes speed, distance, and temperature units for both imperial and metric, covered by a unit test.

items.title: Indicate temperature coupling in the Units section

narrative.Acceptance: The Settings Units section uses the inclusive option labels, a picker row naming temperature, and a footer stating temperature follows this selection.
